title: Week 12 - Integrated Math Algorithm Projects
description: Practice integrated statistics, search, sorting, enumeration, ranking, and best-plan modeling.

questions.title: Class Score Analysis System: Total, Average, and Passing Count

description.background: The teacher stores class scores in the array scores. Now a basic score analysis is needed.
description.task: Calculate the total score, average score, and number of passing students.
description.input: One array scores and one number n.
description.output: Return the total score, average score, and passing count in order.
description.hint: One traversal can complete both summation and counting.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    local sum = 0
    local passCount = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        sum = sum + scores[i]
        if scores[i] >= 60 then
            passCount = passCount + 1
        end
    end

    local average = sum / n
    return sum, average, passCount
end

questions.title: Game Points Analysis System: Highest, Lowest, and Average

description.background: A player's points from multiple games are stored in the array points. The system needs to analyze the highest score, lowest score, and average score.
description.task: Calculate the highest score, lowest score, and average score.
description.input: One array points and one number n.
description.output: Return the highest score, lowest score, and average score in order.
description.hint: The maximum and minimum can be updated in the same traversal.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(points, n)
    local maxValue = points[1]
    local minValue = points[1]
    local sum = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        sum = sum + points[i]

        if points[i] > maxValue then
            maxValue = points[i]
        end

        if points[i] < minValue then
            minValue = points[i]
        end
    end

    return maxValue, minValue, sum / n
end

questions.title: Daily Savings Record System: Total, Highest Day, and Goal Check

description.background: A student's daily savings are stored in the array savings. The student wants to know the total saved, the highest single-day saving amount, and whether the target amount has been reached.
description.task: Calculate the total savings, highest single-day saving, and whether the target is reached.
description.input: One array savings, one number n, and one number target.
description.output: Return the total amount, highest single-day saving, and whether the target is reached in order.
description.hint: Accumulating the total and finding the maximum can be done at the same time.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(savings, n, target)
    local total = 0
    local maxDay = savings[1]

    for i = 1, n do
        total = total + savings[i]

        if savings[i] > maxDay then
            maxDay = savings[i]
        end
    end

    local reached = false
    if total >= target then
        reached = true
    end

    return total, maxDay, reached
end

questions.title: Game Leaderboard System: Sorting, Top Three, and Entry Check

description.background: A game leaderboard stores existing player scores in scores. The system needs to sort the scores from high to low, take the top three, and decide whether newScore can enter the top three.
description.task: Complete leaderboard sorting, top-three extraction, and new-score entry checking.
description.input: One array scores, one number n, and one number newScore. n is guaranteed to be at least 3.
description.output: Return first place, second place, third place, and whether newScore can enter the top three in order.
description.hint: After sorting, third place is the key boundary for entry.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n, newScore)
    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        local maxIndex = i

        for j = i + 1, n do
            if scores[j] > scores[maxIndex] then
                maxIndex = j
            end
        end

        local temp = scores[i]
        scores[i] = scores[maxIndex]
        scores[maxIndex] = temp
    end

    local canEnter = false
    if newScore >= scores[3] then
        canEnter = true
    end

    return scores[1], scores[2], scores[3], canEnter
end

questions.title: Book Number Management System: Search, Sorting, and Classification

description.background: Book numbers in books are originally unordered. The administrator wants to know whether target originally exists, sort the numbers, and count how many numbers are odd and even.
description.task: Find the original position of target, count odd and even numbers, and sort books from small to large.
description.input: One array books, one number n, and one number target.
description.output: Return the original target position, odd count, even count, and sorted books array in order. If target is not found, return 0 for the position.
description.hint: Search and classification can be done first, and sorting can be done afterward.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(books, n, target)
    local position = 0
    local oddCount = 0
    local evenCount = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        if books[i] == target and position == 0 then
            position = i
        end

        if books[i] % 2 == 0 then
            evenCount = evenCount + 1
        else
            oddCount = oddCount + 1
        end
    end

    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        local minIndex = i

        for j = i + 1, n do
            if books[j] < books[minIndex] then
                minIndex = j
            end
        end

        local temp = books[i]
        books[i] = books[minIndex]
        books[minIndex] = temp
    end

    return position, oddCount, evenCount, books
end

questions.title: Sports Day Results System: Best Result, Ranking, and Range Statistics

description.background: Sports day results are stored in the array scores. Higher scores rank earlier. The teacher wants to know the highest score, the rank of targetScore, and how many students are in the high, middle, and needs-improvement ranges.
description.task: Count the range groups, sort scores from high to low, and find the first rank of targetScore.
description.input: One array scores, one number n, and one number targetScore.
description.output: Return the highest score, targetScore rank, high-score count, middle-score count, and needs-improvement count in order. If targetScore is not found, return 0 for the rank.
description.hint: You can classify first, then sort and search for the rank.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n, targetScore)
    local high = 0
    local middle = 0
    local low = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        if scores[i] >= 90 then
            high = high + 1
        elseif scores[i] >= 60 then
            middle = middle + 1
        else
            low = low + 1
        end
    end

    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        for j = 1, n - i do
            if scores[j] < scores[j + 1] then
                local temp = scores[j]
                scores[j] = scores[j + 1]
                scores[j + 1] = temp
            end
        end
    end

    local best = scores[1]
    local rank = 0

    for i = 1, n do
        if scores[i] == targetScore and rank == 0 then
            rank = i
        end
    end

    return best, rank, high, middle, low
end

questions.title: Shopping Plan System: Find the Most Items Within Budget with the Least Leftover

description.background: A store sells two products with prices priceA and priceB. A student has budget money and wants to buy as many total items as possible; if the total item count is tied, the student wants the smallest leftover money.
description.task: Enumerate all purchase plans that do not exceed the budget and find the best plan.
description.input: Three numbers: priceA, priceB, and budget.
description.output: Return product A count, product B count, total item count, and leftover money in order.
description.hint: Split the problem into enumerating plans, checking the budget, comparing quantity, and comparing leftover money.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(priceA, priceB, budget)
    local bestA = 0
    local bestB = 0
    local bestCount = 0
    local bestLeft = budget

    for a = 0, budget do
        for b = 0, budget do
            local cost = a * priceA + b * priceB

            if cost <= budget then
                local count = a + b
                local left = budget - cost

                if count > bestCount then
                    bestCount = count
                    bestLeft = left
                    bestA = a
                    bestB = b
                elseif count == bestCount and left < bestLeft then
                    bestLeft = left
                    bestA = a
                    bestB = b
                end
            end
        end
    end

    return bestA, bestB, bestCount, bestLeft
end

questions.title: Dice Possibility System: Count All Sums and Find the Most Frequent Result

description.background: Two dice both have values from 1 to diceMax. The system needs to count how many times each possible sum appears and find the sum that appears most often.
description.task: Enumerate all outcomes of the two dice, count each sum, and return the most frequent sum and its count.
description.output: Return the most frequent sum and the number of times that sum appears, in order.
description.hint: You can use an array to record how many times each sum appears, then find the largest count.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(diceMax)
    local counts = {}

    for sum = 2, diceMax * 2 do
        counts[sum] = 0
    end

    for a = 1, diceMax do
        for b = 1, diceMax do
            local sum = a + b
            counts[sum] = counts[sum] + 1
        end
    end

    local bestSum = 2
    local bestCount = counts[2]

    for sum = 3, diceMax * 2 do
        if counts[sum] > bestCount then
            bestCount = counts[sum]
            bestSum = sum
        end
    end

    return bestSum, bestCount
end

questions.title: Integrated Score Ranking System: Sorting, Tied Ranking, and Student Rank Search

description.background: Each student in a class has an ID in ids and a score in scores. The teacher needs to sort students by score from high to low; if scores are equal, students share the same rank. Finally, the teacher needs to find the target student's score and rank.
description.task: Sort student IDs and scores together, handle tied ranks, and return the target student's score and rank.
description.input: One student ID array ids, one score array scores, one number n, and one number targetId.
description.output: If the target student is found, return the target student's score and rank in order; if not found, return 0 and 0.
description.hint: When sorting scores, the matching student IDs must be swapped with them. After sorting, calculate tied ranks from adjacent scores.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(ids, scores, n, targetId)
    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        local maxIndex = i

        for j = i + 1, n do
            if scores[j] > scores[maxIndex] then
                maxIndex = j
            end
        end

        local tempScore = scores[i]
        scores[i] = scores[maxIndex]
        scores[maxIndex] = tempScore

        local tempId = ids[i]
        ids[i] = ids[maxIndex]
        ids[maxIndex] = tempId
    end

    local currentRank = 1

    for i = 1, n do
        if i == 1 then
            currentRank = 1
        elseif scores[i] < scores[i - 1] then
            currentRank = i
        end

        if ids[i] == targetId then
            return scores[i], currentRank
        end
    end

    return 0, 0
end
